pairs.subject: Mandatory Safety Training Session
pairs.desc:
Dear Team,

We are implementing a new safety training session that is mandatory for all employees. This training will cover essential safety protocols and emergency procedures. Please review the attached document for details on the training schedule and how to prepare.

Your participation is crucial to ensure a safe work environment for everyone.

Best regards,
HR Team
pairs.explain: The email is from HR and contains a legitimate attachment regarding safety training.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Safety Training Required
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

It has come to our attention that you have not completed the mandatory safety training. Attached is the training document that you must download and execute immediately to avoid penalties.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action.

Sincerely,
HR Department
pairs.explain: The email requests the employee to execute an attachment, which is a common phishing tactic.

pairs.subject: Quarterly Financial Report
pairs.desc:
Hello Team,

Attached you will find the quarterly financial report for our review. Please take the time to go through the document and prepare any questions for our upcoming meeting.

Let’s ensure we are all on the same page regarding our financial performance.

Thank you,
Finance Team
pairs.explain: The email is from the finance department and contains a legitimate report attachment.

pairs.subject: Immediate Action Required: Financial Report
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We have identified discrepancies in your financial submissions. Please find the attached report and execute it to rectify these issues immediately.

Failure to act may lead to serious consequences.

Regards,
Finance Department
pairs.explain: The email prompts the employee to execute a file, which is a phishing indicator.

pairs.subject: IT System Upgrade Notification
pairs.desc:
Dear Team,

We are excited to inform you about an upcoming upgrade to our IT systems. The attached document outlines the changes and how they will benefit your daily tasks.

Please read through it and reach out if you have any questions.

Best,
IT Department
pairs.explain: The email provides information about a system upgrade and includes a legitimate attachment.

pairs.subject: Action Needed: IT System Upgrade
pairs.desc:
Hello,

Due to security concerns, you must download and run the attached file to complete the IT system upgrade. This is mandatory and must be done by the end of the day.

Failure to comply will result in restricted access.

Thank you,
IT Team
pairs.explain: The email instructs the employee to run an executable file, which is a red flag for phishing.

pairs.subject: Travel Itinerary Confirmation
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

Your travel itinerary for the upcoming business trip is attached. Please review the details and let us know if you have any questions or need adjustments.

Safe travels!

Best,
Travel Department
pairs.explain: The email confirms a travel itinerary with a legitimate attachment.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Travel Itinerary Update
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

There has been a critical update to your travel itinerary. Please download and execute the attached file to receive the latest information.

Ignoring this may lead to complications during your trip.

Regards,
Travel Team
pairs.explain: The email asks the employee to execute an attachment, which is a common phishing tactic.

pairs.subject: Legal Document Review
pairs.desc:
Hello,

Attached is a legal document that requires your review. Please provide your feedback by the end of the week.

Your input is valuable to ensure compliance and accuracy.

Best regards,
Legal Team
pairs.explain: The email is from the legal department and contains a legitimate document for review.

pairs.subject: Important: Legal Document Action Required
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

You are required to download and execute the attached legal document to confirm your agreement. This action must be completed immediately to avoid legal issues.

Thank you,
Legal Department
pairs.explain: The email requests the employee to execute a file, which is a phishing tactic.
